{"key_event":"February Revolution and universal male suffrage","reasoning":"Civil society power rose substantially because revolution, banquet networks, newspapers, and universal male suffrage transformed mobilization and formal participation, though the June Days repression limited working-class organization. State power remained strong through centralized administration, policing, infrastructure, and June coercion, but the February collapse of the July Monarchy and regime instability left it somewhat below the prior period.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":7.4,"state_change":-0.3,"state_power":7.5}
